verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est nomen bonum quam unguenta pretiosa,  et dies mortis die nativitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֥וֹב שֵׁ֖ם מִשֶּׁ֣מֶן ט֑וֹב וְי֣וֹם הַמָּ֔וֶת מִיּ֖וֹם הִוָּלְדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A good name is better than precious ointment; and the day of death than the day of one’s birth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est ire ad domum luctus  quam ad domum convivii ;  in illa enim finis cunctorum admonetur hominum,  et vivens cogitat quid futurum sit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֞וֹב לָלֶ֣כֶת אֶל־בֵּֽית־אֵ֗בֶל מִלֶּ֙כֶת֙ אֶל־בֵּ֣ית מִשְׁתֶּ֔ה בַּאֲשֶׁ֕ר ה֖וּא ס֣וֹף כׇּל־הָאָדָ֑ם וְהַחַ֖י יִתֵּ֥ן אֶל־לִבּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: It is better to go to the house of mourning, than to go to the house of feasting: for that is the end of all men; and the living will lay it to his heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est ira risu,  quia per tristitiam vultus corrigitur animus delinquentis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֥וֹב כַּ֖עַס מִשְּׂח֑וֹק כִּֽי־בְרֹ֥עַ פָּנִ֖ים יִ֥יטַב לֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: Sorrow is better than laughter: for by the sadness of the countenance the heart is made better.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor sapientium ubi tristitia est,  et cor stultorum ubi lætitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֤ב חֲכָמִים֙ בְּבֵ֣ית אֵ֔בֶל וְלֵ֥ב כְּסִילִ֖ים בְּבֵ֥ית שִׂמְחָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning; but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est a sapiente corripi,  quam stultorum adulatione decipi ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֕וֹב לִשְׁמֹ֖עַ גַּעֲרַ֣ת חָכָ֑ם מֵאִ֕ישׁ שֹׁמֵ֖עַ שִׁ֥יר כְּסִילִֽים׃
verses.king_james: It is better to hear the rebuke of the wise, than for a man to hear the song of fools.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia sicut sonitus spinarum ardentium sub olla,  sic risus stulti.  Sed et hoc vanitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י כְק֤וֹל הַסִּירִים֙ תַּ֣חַת הַסִּ֔יר כֵּ֖ן שְׂחֹ֣ק הַכְּסִ֑יל וְגַם־זֶ֖ה הָֽבֶל׃
verses.king_james: For as the crackling of thorns under a pot, so is the laughter of the fool: this also is vanity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Calumnia conturbat sapientem,  et perdet robur cordis illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֥י הָעֹ֖שֶׁק יְהוֹלֵ֣ל חָכָ֑ם וִֽיאַבֵּ֥ד אֶת־לֵ֖ב מַתָּנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Surely oppression maketh a wise man mad; and a gift destroyeth the heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est finis orationis quam principium.  Melior est patiens arrogante.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֛וֹב אַחֲרִ֥ית דָּבָ֖ר מֵֽרֵאשִׁית֑וֹ ט֥וֹב אֶֽרֶךְ־ר֖וּחַ מִגְּבַהּ־רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof: and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne sis velox ad irascendum,  quia ira in sinu stulti requiescit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תְּבַהֵ֥ל בְּרֽוּחֲךָ֖ לִכְע֑וֹס כִּ֣י כַ֔עַס בְּחֵ֥יק כְּסִילִ֖ים יָנֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: Be not hasty in thy spirit to be angry: for anger resteth in the bosom of fools.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dicas : Quid putas causæ est  quod priora tempora meliora fuere quam nunc sunt ?  stulta enim est hujuscemodi interrogatio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תֹּאמַר֙ מֶ֣ה הָיָ֔ה שֶׁ֤הַיָּמִים֙ הָרִ֣אשֹׁנִ֔ים הָי֥וּ טוֹבִ֖ים מֵאֵ֑לֶּה כִּ֛י לֹ֥א מֵחׇכְמָ֖ה שָׁאַ֥לְתָּ עַל־זֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Say not thou, What is the cause that the former days were better than these? for thou dost not enquire wisely concerning this.

verses.latin_vulgate: Utilior est sapientia cum divitiis,  et magis prodest videntibus solem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טוֹבָ֥ה חׇכְמָ֖ה עִֽם־נַחֲלָ֑ה וְיֹתֵ֖ר לְרֹאֵ֥י הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Wisdom is good with an inheritance: and by it there is profit to them that see the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim protegit sapientia, sic protegit pecunia ;  hoc autem plus habet eruditio et sapientia,  quod vitam tribuunt possessori suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י בְּצֵ֥ל הַֽחׇכְמָ֖ה בְּצֵ֣ל הַכָּ֑סֶף וְיִתְר֣וֹן דַּ֔עַת הַֽחׇכְמָ֖ה תְּחַיֶּ֥ה בְעָלֶֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: For wisdom is a defence, and money is a defence: but the excellency of knowledge is, that wisdom giveth life to them that have it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Considera opera Dei,  quod nemo possit corrigere quem ille despexerit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְאֵ֖ה אֶת־מַעֲשֵׂ֣ה הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים כִּ֣י מִ֤י יוּכַל֙ לְתַקֵּ֔ן אֵ֖ת אֲשֶׁ֥ר עִוְּתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Consider the work of God: for who can make that straight, which he hath made crooked?

verses.latin_vulgate: In die bona fruere bonis,  et malam diem præcave ;  sicut enim hanc, sic et illam fecit Deus,  ut non inveniat homo contra eum justas querimonias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּי֤וֹם טוֹבָה֙ הֱיֵ֣ה בְט֔וֹב וּבְי֥וֹם רָעָ֖ה רְאֵ֑ה גַּ֣ם אֶת־זֶ֤ה לְעֻמַּת־זֶה֙ עָשָׂ֣ה הָֽאֱלֹהִ֔ים עַל־דִּבְרַ֗ת שֶׁלֹּ֨א יִמְצָ֧א הָֽאָדָ֛ם אַחֲרָ֖יו מְאֽוּמָה׃
verses.king_james: In the day of prosperity be joyful, but in the day of adversity consider: God also hath set the one over against the other, to the end that man should find nothing after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc quoque vidi in diebus vanitatis meæ :  justus perit in justitia sua,  et impius multo vivit tempore in malitia sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־הַכֹּ֥ל רָאִ֖יתִי בִּימֵ֣י הֶבְלִ֑י יֵ֤שׁ צַדִּיק֙ אֹבֵ֣ד בְּצִדְק֔וֹ וְיֵ֣שׁ רָשָׁ֔ע מַאֲרִ֖יךְ בְּרָעָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: All things have I seen in the days of my vanity: there is a just man that perisheth in his righteousness, and there is a wicked man that prolongeth his life in his wickedness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli esse justus multum,  neque plus sapias quam necesse est,  ne obstupescas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תְּהִ֤י צַדִּיק֙ הַרְבֵּ֔ה וְאַל־תִּתְחַכַּ֖ם יוֹתֵ֑ר לָ֖מָּה תִּשּׁוֹמֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: Be not righteous over much; neither make thyself over wise: why shouldest thou destroy thyself?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne impie agas multum,  et noli esse stultus,  ne moriaris in tempore non tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּרְשַׁ֥ע הַרְבֵּ֖ה וְאַל־תְּהִ֣י סָכָ֑ל לָ֥מָּה תָמ֖וּת בְּלֹ֥א עִתֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Be not over much wicked, neither be thou foolish: why shouldest thou die before thy time?

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum est te sustentare justum :  sed et ab illo ne subtrahas manum tuam ;  quia qui timet Deum nihil negligit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֚וֹב אֲשֶׁ֣ר תֶּאֱחֹ֣ז בָּזֶ֔ה וְגַם־מִזֶּ֖ה אַל־תַּנַּ֣ח אֶת־יָדֶ֑ךָ כִּֽי־יְרֵ֥א אֱלֹהִ֖ים יֵצֵ֥א אֶת־כֻּלָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: It is good that thou shouldest take hold of this; yea, also from this withdraw not thine hand: for he that feareth God shall come forth of them all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia confortavit sapientem  super decem principes civitatis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽחׇכְמָ֖ה תָּעֹ֣ז לֶחָכָ֑ם מֵֽעֲשָׂרָה֙ שַׁלִּיטִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר הָי֖וּ בָּעִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: Wisdom strengtheneth the wise more than ten mighty men which are in the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: non est enim homo justus in terra  qui faciat bonum et non peccet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י אָדָ֔ם אֵ֥ין צַדִּ֖יק בָּאָ֑רֶץ אֲשֶׁ֥ר יַעֲשֶׂה־טּ֖וֹב וְלֹ֥א יֶחֱטָֽא׃
verses.king_james: For there is not a just man upon earth, that doeth good, and sinneth not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et cunctis sermonibus qui dicuntur  ne accomodes cor tuum,  ne forte audias servum tuum maledicentem tibi ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֤ם לְכׇל־הַדְּבָרִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר יְדַבֵּ֔רוּ אַל־תִּתֵּ֖ן לִבֶּ֑ךָ אֲשֶׁ֛ר לֹֽא־תִשְׁמַ֥ע אֶֽת־עַבְדְּךָ֖ מְקַלְלֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Also take no heed unto all words that are spoken; lest thou hear thy servant curse thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: scit enim conscientia tua  quia et tu crebro maledixisti aliis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י גַּם־פְּעָמִ֥ים רַבּ֖וֹת יָדַ֣ע לִבֶּ֑ךָ אֲשֶׁ֥ר גַּם־[אַתָּ֖ה] (את) קִלַּ֥לְתָּ אֲחֵרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: For oftentimes also thine own heart knoweth that thou thyself likewise hast cursed others.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cuncta tentavi in sapientia.  Dixi : Sapiens efficiar :  et ipsa longius recessit a me,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־זֹ֖ה נִסִּ֣יתִי בַֽחׇכְמָ֑ה אָמַ֣רְתִּי אֶחְכָּ֔מָה וְהִ֖יא רְחוֹקָ֥ה מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.king_james: All this have I proved by wisdom: I said, I will be wise; but it was far from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: multo magis quam erat.  Et alta profunditas, quis inveniet eam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָח֖וֹק מַה־שֶּׁהָיָ֑ה וְעָמֹ֥ק ׀ עָמֹ֖ק מִ֥י יִמְצָאֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: That which is far off, and exceeding deep, who can find it out?

verses.latin_vulgate: Lustravi universa animo meo,  ut scirem et considerarem,  et quærerem sapientiam, et rationem,  et ut cognoscerem impietatem stulti,  et errorem imprudentium :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סַבּ֨וֹתִֽי אֲנִ֤י וְלִבִּי֙ לָדַ֣עַת וְלָת֔וּר וּבַקֵּ֥שׁ חׇכְמָ֖ה וְחֶשְׁבּ֑וֹן וְלָדַ֙עַת֙ רֶ֣שַׁע כֶּ֔סֶל וְהַסִּכְל֖וּת הוֹלֵלֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: I applied mine heart to know, and to search, and to seek out wisdom, and the reason of things, and to know the wickedness of folly, even of foolishness and madness:

verses.latin_vulgate: et inveni amariorem morte mulierem,  quæ laqueus venatorum est,  et sagena cor ejus ;  vincula sunt manus illius.  Qui placet Deo effugiet illam ;  qui autem peccator est capietur ab illa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמוֹצֶ֨א אֲנִ֜י מַ֣ר מִמָּ֗וֶת אֶת־הָֽאִשָּׁה֙ אֲשֶׁר־הִ֨יא מְצוֹדִ֧ים וַחֲרָמִ֛ים לִבָּ֖הּ אֲסוּרִ֣ים יָדֶ֑יהָ ט֞וֹב לִפְנֵ֤י הָאֱלֹהִים֙ יִמָּלֵ֣ט מִמֶּ֔נָּה וְחוֹטֵ֖א יִלָּ֥כֶד בָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: And I find more bitter than death the woman, whose heart is snares and nets, and her hands as bands: whoso pleaseth God shall escape from her; but the sinner shall be taken by her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce hoc inveni, dixit Ecclesiastes,  unum et alterum ut invenirem rationem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְאֵה֙ זֶ֣ה מָצָ֔אתִי אָמְרָ֖ה קֹהֶ֑לֶת אַחַ֥ת לְאַחַ֖ת לִמְצֹ֥א חֶשְׁבּֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Behold, this have I found, saith the preacher, counting one by one, to find out the account:

verses.latin_vulgate: quam adhuc quærit anima mea,  et non inveni.  Virum de mille unum reperi ;  mulierem ex omnibus non inveni.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֛ר עוֹד־בִּקְשָׁ֥ה נַפְשִׁ֖י וְלֹ֣א מָצָ֑אתִי אָדָ֞ם אֶחָ֤ד מֵאֶ֙לֶף֙ מָצָ֔אתִי וְאִשָּׁ֥ה בְכׇל־אֵ֖לֶּה לֹ֥א מָצָֽאתִי׃
verses.king_james: Which yet my soul seeketh, but I find not: one man among a thousand have I found; but a woman among all those have I not found.

verses.latin_vulgate: Solummodo hoc inveni,  quod fecerit Deus hominem rectum,  et ipse se infinitis miscuerit quæstionibus.  Quis talis ut sapiens est ?  et quis cognovit solutionem verbi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְבַד֙ רְאֵה־זֶ֣ה מָצָ֔אתִי אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָשָׂ֧ה הָאֱלֹהִ֛ים אֶת־הָאָדָ֖ם יָשָׁ֑ר וְהֵ֥מָּה בִקְשׁ֖וּ חִשְּׁבֹנ֥וֹת רַבִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Lo, this only have I found, that God hath made man upright; but they have sought out many inventions.
